description: Algorithm-stability of the e-0049 config-null DEPLETION ratio (not absolute counts). For each of the 5 community-detection partitions e-0052 used (leiden_modularity, louvain, fastgreedy, label_propagation, infomap) over the SAME deterministically-sorted LCC (92,594 nodes), recompute the e-0049 degree-preserving (configuration-model) null ENTIRELY within that partition's pure-cluster universe: observed bridge rate = P(a theorem's actual premises span >=2 distinct pure clusters); USAGE/TYPE null rate = closed-form E[same] when each theorem's k premises are redrawn iid from the usage/type-weighted global premise distribution; enrichment = observed/null (<1 => depletion). The ONLY thing varying across rows is the partition.
motivation: e-0052 (a-0052) showed the operational-bridge population is NOT algorithm-invariant in absolute scale or named identity, but explicitly flagged: 'counts here are raw, not null-normalized; whether the ~16-20x depletion-vs-null itself is algorithm-stable is untested and a natural follow-up.' e-0049's depletion is a RELATIVE per-partition statistic (observed/null at matched premise counts) that could survive even when absolute counts do not, because a finer partition inflates BOTH observed and null rates. CPU-only, no new data.
formal_results: 5 algorithms over the sorted LCC (92,594 nodes). Per-partition observed bridge rate / USAGE-null rate / usage-depletion factor (null/observed): leiden_modularity (138 clusters, 112 pure) 0.531% / 8.736% / 16.46x; louvain (198, 171) 1.051% / 10.566% / 10.05x; fastgreedy (1106, 1032) 0.176% / 2.263% / 12.83x; label_propagation (816, 781) 1.330% / 4.715% / 3.55x; infomap (4190, 3081) 41.198% / 56.913% / 1.38x. all_partitions_depleted=true (every enrichment_usage<1). Usage-depletion range 1.38-16.46x, mean 8.85x; TYPE-depletion range 1.63-47.08x, mean 20.09x.
results_description: The DIRECTION of the e-0049 result is algorithm-INVARIANT: all 5 partitions show DEPLETION (observed cross-domain bridging strictly below a degree-preserving null), so 'premises are domain-LOCAL' holds under every clustering algorithm, not just Leiden. But the MAGNITUDE is resolution-dependent and decays monotonically as the partition gets finer: 16.46x (leiden, 138 clusters) -> 10.05x (louvain, 198) -> 12.83x (fastgreedy, 1106) -> 3.55x (label_prop, 816) -> 1.38x (infomap, 4190). At infomap's ~4k-cluster resolution ~41% of the graph is already an observed bridge and the null expects ~57%, so the ratio collapses toward 1. The strong ~16-20x regime is specific to the coarse modularity resolution (leiden/louvain), exactly the regime e-0052 found the canonical object lives in.
conclusions: Refines e-0052/a-0052 and underpins e-0049/a-0043. The qualitative claim behind q-0006/q-0007 -- that true operational bridges are RARITIES defying strong premise locality -- is algorithm-ROBUST in DIRECTION (all 5 algorithms: depletion, never enrichment), so domain-locality of premises is not a Leiden artifact. But the specific ~16-20x DEPLETION FACTOR is NOT algorithm-stable: it ranges 1.38-16.46x (usage) and weakens monotonically with finer resolution, reaching near-parity (1.38x) at infomap's fine partition. So the depletion result is more robust than e-0052's absolute scale/identity (which spanned 98-23,134 with empty cross-algo core) -- direction survives where count and identity did not -- but its headline magnitude is still a modularity-resolution number. Both the named bridge lists AND the depletion magnitude should be read relative to the ~140-200-cluster modularity regime.
